teacher_themes.label: Classroom Mental Health Literacy & Early Identification
teacher_themes.explanation: Districts want teachers to recognize early warning signs of mental health struggles, including anxiety, depression, and suicide risk, and understand how MH impacts classroom behavior.

teacher_themes.label: Tier 1 Classroom Tools & Downloadable Resources
teacher_themes.explanation: Several districts requested practical, ready-to-use classroom resources such as handouts, sticker charts, de-escalation tools, landing pages, and monthly resource communities for teachers.

teacher_themes.label: PD / Professional Learning Sessions for Teachers
teacher_themes.explanation: Districts expressed interest in formal professional development or professional learning sessions, ranging from 1-hour PD blocks to embedded PBIS sessions and back-to-school assemblies.

teacher_themes.label: Support for Students with IEPs & Special Needs
teacher_themes.explanation: At least one district specifically requested targeted teacher content (1-pagers, behavior guides) for students with IEPs and special needs.

teacher_themes.label: Phased / Deferred Rollout to Teachers via Counselors or Principals
teacher_themes.explanation: Multiple districts are not yet engaging teachers directly and plan to route resources through counselors, principals, or school social workers first before expanding to teachers.

comms_channels.channel: Staff newsletter cascaded to family newsletter

feedback_themes.theme: Communication gaps around referral status, no-shows, and session notifications
examples: Horizon Charters wants alerts when students no-show and clearer referral status updates.
Jefferson County needs all services triaged through counselors with better scheduling communication.
Snowline staff frustrated by lack of appointment notifications during school hours, unlike prior provider.
Kipp Nor Cal: 57 referrals but only 7 in care; need better dashboard monitoring and conversion support.

feedback_themes.theme: Positive year-one partnership experience and growth momentum
examples: Chicago Heights 170: first year has been good; Daybreak is a viable and helpful resource.
Lammersville Joint looking forward to year two with positive tone.
Escalon referrals went up significantly compared to previous year; district is pleased.
Rockingham County appreciates quick response and ongoing support.

feedback_themes.theme: Need for more teacher and family-facing resources and marketing content
examples: Escondido Union wants a dedicated teacher landing page with downloadable classroom resources.
Oconee County requests materials on school avoidance and anxiety parents don't recognize.
Adelanto Elementary says marketing content needs to feel more friendly and approachable.
White County wants paper flyers to hand out at food drives and back-to-school events.

feedback_themes.theme: Utilization and access concerns (conversion rates, insurance, on-site competition)
examples: Fulton County unhappy that 6 students signed up but only 1 received services given timeline.
Belmont-Redwood Shores requests more transparency on denied self-funded insurance plans.
Apple Valley anticipates utilization drop as they now have on-site clinicians.
Redding started late in the year; expects much higher access next year.
